denial_letter_text:
Dear Mr. Member,

Aetna has completed a medical necessity review of the request for the requested cancer therapy and is unable to approve it at this time.

Under the applicable oncology utilization criteria for immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy, coverage requires documentation of measurable or unresectable disease, or radiographically confirmed metastatic involvement, before therapy can be authorized. The submitted records do not establish disease meeting these threshold requirements, and the request therefore does not satisfy the criteria set referenced for this review.

The information previously submitted on your behalf, including any provider correspondence, did not include sufficient detail to establish that the treatment is medically necessary under these criteria, and the standard criteria apply to this determination.

If you disagree with this decision, you may submit a written appeal to:
Aetna Pharmacy Appeals Unit
P.O. Box 14579
Lexington, KY 40512
Fax: (859) 455-8650

Appeals must be received within the timeframe described in your plan documents. You may also have the right to request an external review by an independent review organization.

This determination concerns coverage under your plan and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
Aetna Clinical Review

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.
clinical_context: The patient is a 34-year-old man with stage IIIB cutaneous melanoma. A 2.6 mm Breslow-depth primary on the left shoulder was treated with wide local excision achieving clear margins, and sentinel lymph node biopsy was positive in one of three nodes; staging imaging showed no distant metastatic disease. Molecular testing is BRAF wild-type. His medical oncologist recommended adjuvant pembrolizumab, the standard of care for resected high-risk stage III melanoma, to reduce the substantial risk of recurrence. Before the determination, the oncologist submitted a detailed letter of medical necessity citing the resected stage IIIB pathology, the positive sentinel node, and the NCCN category 1 recommendation for adjuvant anti-PD-1 therapy in this setting, and explaining that adjuvant treatment is given precisely because there is no measurable residual disease after complete resection. That letter, along with the operative and pathology reports, was part of the submitted file. There is no documented contraindication to immunotherapy, and the patient's ECOG performance status is 0.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches the assigned cell field-for-field; C43.9 melanoma is oncology; adjuvant pembrolizumab realistic; rationale instantiates guideline_mis_cite (metastatic criteria applied to adjuvant setting); age 34 in band.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Aetna denying adjuvant pembrolizumab by misapplying measurable/metastatic-disease ICI criteria to a resected adjuvant case is an authentic wrong-module mis-cite; misrouted pharmacy appeal and dismissed LMN are believable procedural defects.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Correctly branded Aetna with cool procedural cadence and no wrong-insurer tells, but criteria left generic rather than naming an Aetna CPB / InterQual.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy guideline_mis_cite: cites a real class of ICI UM criteria, applies the metastatic threshold to an adjuvant case, never ties it to the member's records; circular and appealable, no hallucinated named guideline.

clinical_realism.reasoning: 2.6 mm Breslow primary, one positive sentinel node, no distant disease maps to stage IIIB; BRAF wild-type drives anti-PD-1; adjuvant pembrolizumab is NCCN cat 1; ECOG 0. No biology violations.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Adjuvant pembrolizumab is FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed therapy for resected stage III melanoma; clean match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: Melanoma C43.9 / adjuvant pembrolizumab / resected curative-intent trajectory and guideline_mis_cite shared by no neighbour (all metastatic/recurrent, different drugs).

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.067 (nearest=case_109_aetna_mednec), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: No banned topics; curative-intent adjuvant therapy (not end-of-life); adult 34M; commercial Aetna, no government-payer framing.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender, diagnosis, treatment stable across documents; the measurable-disease requirement vs resected no-measurable-disease is the appealable core (guideline misapplied), not an unstable scenario fact; internally consistent.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal insurer boilerplate plus dense human-style chart prose; no GPT vocabulary, listicles, or meta-commentary.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Aetna register with realistic obfuscation; clinical context plain factual narrative, no pleading.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability (standard practice); no dollar figures; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: No invented statutes, no Medicare framing; appeal window deliberately vague and ERISA disclosures omitted as realistic attackable imperfection; named appeals destination.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 34M with cutaneous melanoma is epidemiologically sound; consistent male pronouns and 'Mr.' salutation; no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Aetna in-scope, fully-insured commercial, adult 34, member-facing appeal rights only, no live-filing/representation language.

date_sanity.reasoning: No explicit dates in letter or clinical context; no contradictions or impossible durations, but no affirmatively dated timeline either — broadly OK but vague.
date_sanity.improvement: Add a determination date and a dated clinical milestone.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Generic un-named criteria references and no hallucinated alphanumeric codes; realistically vague umbrella citation.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Metastatic/measurable-disease criteria misapplied to an adjuvant request (category error); false 'insufficient detail' claim despite a detailed LMN + op/path reports; ignores NCCN cat 1; misrouted appeal. Easy multi-vector appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Wrong criteria applied: metastatic/measurable-disease ICI criteria used to deny an adjuvant request, where absence of measurable disease is the point of treatment
False factual claim that records lacked detail despite a detailed letter of medical necessity, operative, and pathology reports being submitted
Ignores NCCN category 1 recommendation for adjuvant anti-PD-1 in resected high-risk stage III melanoma; appeal misrouted to the Pharmacy Appeals Unit (wrong department) which can extend the deadline
strong_defenses: The literal text of the cited ICI utilization criterion does require documentation of measurable, unresectable, or metastatic disease, which this fully resected patient does not have, giving the insurer a facially consistent though misapplied threshold to point to
